# Betti Mesh Guide

Parent: [Gnosis Polyglot](../README.md)

Scheduling certified GG topologies across the deployed Betti worker fleet:
the `/schedule` contract, arm evidence, budget-bounded long arms with cursor
resume, width governance and shed, race mode, decline propagation, and token
burn. All live outputs in this doc were captured against the deployed fleet
on 2026-07-03.

Companions: [BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md) (writing and
compiling artifacts), [BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md](./BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md)
(envelope and cursor formats),
[BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md) (how the burn settles into
money). Operator view: [BETTI_FLEET_OPERATIONS.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_FLEET_OPERATIONS.md)
(deploys, health matrix, scaling) and the betti-mesh entries in
[SOVEREIGN-MESH-ZOO.md](../../../../SOVEREIGN-MESH-ZOO.md).

## 1. The fleet

| Worker | URL | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `betti-mesh` | https://betti-mesh.taylorbuley.workers.dev | coordinator: `GET /health`, `POST /schedule` |
| `betti-station-a` / `-b` | https://betti-station-{a,b}.taylorbuley.workers.dev | uniform stations: `GET /health`, `POST /execute {envelope}` — every station runs the SAME generic gg-walker artifact (CompiledWasm import; asset source `x-gnosis/src/workers/assets/gg-walker.wasm`), cron-warmed every 2 minutes |
| `betti-wasm-demo` | https://betti-wasm-demo.taylorbuley.workers.dev | single-isolate artifact execution (not mesh-scheduled); see the [guide](./BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md#63-the-deployed-demo-worker-edge-pre-compiled-artifacts) |

Source: `x-gnosis/src/workers/betti-mesh-worker.ts` +
`x-gnosis/src/mesh-schedule.ts` (scheduling model) +
`x-gnosis/src/density-governor.ts` (width governance); deploy anchors under
`x-gnosis/workers/betti-*/`.

```console
$ curl https://betti-mesh.taylorbuley.workers.dev/health
{"ok":true,"stations":["STATION_A","STATION_B"],"rendezvousR2":true}
```

## 2. What schedules: the certified foil-fanout class

The mesh recognizes exactly one class: a foil fork/fold — a `Promise.all`
Fork/Fold node fanning out to callee branches, folded by `merge` with the
`"|"` join. The coordinator splits it into width-1 arm sub-topologies
(`splitFoilForkArms`), ships each arm as a gg-walker json-v1 envelope to a
station over a service binding, and re-applies the fold itself. No per-arm
compilation happens anywhere — the program is data to the one walker.

Everything else refuses:

```console
$ curl -X POST https://betti-mesh.taylorbuley.workers.dev/schedule \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -d '{"topology":{"nodes":[],"edges":[]},"input":{}}'
HTTP 500
{"error":"mesh schedule refused: topology is outside the certified
foil-fanout class (Promise.all Fork/Fold with merge) — the single-isolate
walker path serves it"}
```

## 3. POST /schedule

Request body:

```json
{
  "topology": { "nodes": [...], "edges": [...] },   // required: fanout GgTopology
  "helpers":  { "<name>": { ... } },                // optional (envelope v2)
  "input":    { "userId": "ada" },                  // required program input
  "mode":     "all" | "race",                       // optional, default "all"
  "budget":   3                                     // optional per-hop arm budget (v3)
}
```

A convenient fixture:
`polyglot/gg-walker-wasm/tests/fixtures/fanout.gg.topology.json`. Live run:

```console
$ curl -sD- -X POST https://betti-mesh.taylorbuley.workers.dev/schedule \
    -H 'content-type: application/json' \
    -d '{"topology": <fanout fixture>, "input": {"userId":"ada"}}'
HTTP/2 200
x-token-burn: 2

{"scheduleId":"ea00bd02-165d-46d7-bacb-108ea3eaf775",
 "result":"ada:user|ada:profile",
 "mode":"all",
 "arms":[
   {"armIndex":0,"callee":"loadUser","payload":"ada:user",
    "evidence":{"station":"betti-station-a","isolateId":"9ee2b061-...","ms":0}},
   {"armIndex":1,"callee":"loadProfile","payload":"ada:profile",
    "evidence":{"station":"betti-station-b","isolateId":"dbdc0b10-...","ms":0}}],
 "plan":{"requested":2,"carrier":10,"wire":800,"cost":1024,
         "effectiveSidecars":2,"binding":"carrier",
         "absorbed":0.13875598086124397,"tokenBurn":2},
 "totalMs":4}
```

Response fields:

- `scheduleId` — UUID for this schedule (also the R2 rendezvous key prefix).
- `result` — the folded answer, byte-equal to what single-isolate gnexec
  produces for the whole topology (`ada:user|ada:profile` in branch order).
- `arms[]` — one entry per dispatched arm: `armIndex`, `callee`, `payload`
  (the arm's own answer), and `evidence` — the executing station's name and
  per-isolate UUID plus its self-reported milliseconds. Distinct isolate ids
  on distinct stations are the proof the work actually spread.
- `plan` — the density-governor admission plan (section 6): the requested
  width, the three ceilings (`carrier`, `wire`, `cost`), which ceiling bound
  (`binding`), the admitted width (`effectiveSidecars`), and `tokenBurn`.
- `totalMs` — coordinator wall time.

Headers: `x-token-burn` mirrors `plan.tokenBurn` (section 8).

## 4. Race mode

`"mode": "race"` dispatches all arms and answers with the first to finish;
the response carries only the winning arm. Losing arms are not cancelled
(Workers service-binding fetches run to completion); the plan and burn are
unchanged — you pay for the width you forked, not the width you kept.

```console
$ curl ... -d '{"topology": <fanout fixture>, "input":{"userId":"ada"}, "mode":"race"}'
{"scheduleId":"cd614dc5-...","result":"ada:profile","mode":"race",
 "arms":[{"armIndex":1,"callee":"loadProfile","payload":"ada:profile",
          "evidence":{"station":"betti-station-b","isolateId":"dbdc0b10-...","ms":0}}],
 "plan":{...,"tokenBurn":2},"totalMs":4}
```

## 5. Decline propagation

The mesh never answers where the single-isolate walker would not. If ANY
arm's walker traps (station answers `422`), the whole schedule declines with
`422` — there is no partial fold, no "best arms" answer. In race mode a
declined arm cannot win; if every arm declines, the schedule declines.
Callers get the same contract as everywhere else in the fabric
([decline = trap](./BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md#7-what-decline--trap-means-for-you-as-a-caller)):
fall back to running the whole topology through the interpreter.

Refusals are distinct from declines: a topology outside the fanout class is
refused `500` before any dispatch (section 2), and an over-width plan sheds
`429` (section 6). Neither is settled as burn.

## 6. Width governance and shed

Before dispatch, the density governor (`planSidecarFanout`) prices the
requested width against three ceilings — CARRIER (coprime fin lanes), WIRE
(bandwidth), and COST (budget over `costPerCall`) — and returns the plan you
see in the response. If the admitted width (`effectiveSidecars`) is narrower
than the topology's fork width, the mesh does NOT partially schedule: it
sheds the whole request with `429` and the plan attached, un-settled. The
live schedules above show `requested: 2` against ceilings `carrier 10 / wire
800 / cost 1024`, binding `carrier`.

## 7. Long arms: budget, cursors, rendezvous (B3)

An optional `budget` (hops per station call, envelope v3) turns each arm
into a cursor loop: the station walks until the budget exhausts and returns
a `__gg_cursor__` ([format](./BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md#61-cursor-format));
the coordinator re-dispatches the cursor as a `resume` envelope — possibly
onto a DIFFERENT station — until the arm completes. Live run with
`"budget": 1`:

```console
$ curl ... -d '{"topology": <fanout fixture>, "input":{"userId":"ada"}, "budget":1}'
HTTP/2 200
x-token-burn: 2

{"scheduleId":"04e0c128-...","result":"ada:user|ada:profile","mode":"all",
 "arms":[
   {"armIndex":0,"callee":"loadUser","payload":"ada:user",
    "evidence":{"station":"betti-station-a","isolateId":"260cb84c-...","ms":0},
    "resumeHops":3,
    "cursorHops":[
      {"ordinal":0,"cursorOrdinal":1,"evidence":{"station":"betti-station-a",...}},
      {"ordinal":1,"cursorOrdinal":2,"evidence":{"station":"betti-station-a",...}},
      {"ordinal":2,"cursorOrdinal":3,"evidence":{"station":"betti-station-a",...}}]},
   {"armIndex":1,...,"resumeHops":3,"cursorHops":[...]}],
 "plan":{...,"tokenBurn":2},"totalMs":76}
```

- `resumeHops` — how many suspend/resume round-trips the arm took.
- `cursorHops[]` — per-hop audit trail: the hop `ordinal`, the cursor's
  walk ordinal after the hop (`cursorOrdinal`, strictly monotone), and the
  evidence of the station that served that hop.
- The final `result` is byte-equal to the unbudgeted run — budgets change
  the execution shape, never the answer. Cross-station resume is pinned
  live (a walk suspended on station-a resumed on station-b; see the Wave C
  addendum in [SOVEREIGN-MESH-ZOO.md](../../../../SOVEREIGN-MESH-ZOO.md)).
- Resume ceiling: 32 resumes per arm (`DEFAULT_MAX_RESUMES`); exceeding it
  answers `429` with the offending `armIndex` and its resume count — a
  budget so small it cannot finish is shed, not spun forever.
- Rendezvous audit lane (optional R2 binding `BETTI_MESH_R2`): every
  intermediate cursor persists fire-and-forget as
  `betti-mesh/rendezvous/<scheduleId>/<armIndex>/<ordinal>.json`. The worker
  runs identically without the binding; `/health` reports `rendezvousR2`.

## 8. Token burn

`tokenBurn = effectiveSidecars * costPerCall` — metered on every admitted
schedule, reported in `plan.tokenBurn` and the `x-token-burn` response
header (live runs above: `x-token-burn: 2` for a width-2 fanout). Shed
(`429`) and refused (`500`) requests are not settled. Burn units settle into
cents through the fail-open settlement seam; the money path (rates, ledgers,
what you are actually charged) is
[BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_PUBLISH_GUIDE.md) and, operator-side,
[BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md).

## 9. swerve as a mesh client (B4)

`swerve` drives the live mesh from the command line. Scheduling takes a GG
topology JSON (the mesh schedules topologies, not compiled artifacts):

```console
$ swerve cline --backend wasm --target mesh --export app \
    --input-json '{"userId":"ada"}' fanout.gg.topology.json
swerve: cline admitted (braid cert 93e1e4e589080b91)
swerve: POST https://betti-mesh.taylorbuley.workers.dev/schedule
{ "arms": [ ... evidence on betti-station-a / betti-station-b ... ],
  "result": "ada:user|ada:profile", ... }
```

Pointing `--target mesh` at a SOURCE file instead emits the Tier A artifact
and adopts it into the local mesh registry (`~/.swerve/mesh-registry/`), but
answers `"schedulable": false` — Tier A artifacts run on the demo worker
surface, not the station walkers; the message tells you to schedule a
topology JSON instead. `swerve mesh-status` is the read-only reconciler:
desired (local registry) vs actual (live `/health` + `/wasm/artifacts`),
printing drift and the manual fix commands; v0 applies nothing.

## 10. Landing

- Consumer product wave (in development): `edgework deploy` CLI verb,
  `/api/v1/functions`, and the `fn.edgework.ai` gateway in front of this
  fleet. Not deployed; do not probe or depend on it yet.

all curls in this doc ran against the deployed fleet that day, and the swerve
transcript against the live mesh. Backing test suites:
`x-gnosis/src/__tests__/{mesh-schedule,mesh-schedule-cursor-loop,betti-mesh-worker,betti-station-cursor-resume}.test.ts`,
`polyglot/gg-walker-wasm/tests/cursor_resume.rs`, swerve mesh tests
(`swerve/src/mesh.rs` suite including the live e2e).
